Stunfisk

#0617 Accelgor #0619 Mienfoo
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Stunfisk is a Ground/Electric type Pokémon introduced in Generation 5.

Stunfisk has a Galarian Form, introduced in Pokémon Sword/Shield.

Stunfisk Galarian Stunfisk

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0618
Type Ground Electric
Species Trap Pokémon
Height 0.7 m (2′04″)
Weight 11.0 kg (24.3 lbs)
Abilities 1. Static
2. Limber
Sand Veil (hidden ability)
Local № 0124 (Black/White)
0231 (Black 2/White 2)
0039 (X/Y — Mountain Kalos)
0226 (Sword/Shield)
0157 (Legends: Z-A)

Training

EV yield 2 HP
Catch rate 75 (9.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 70 (higher than normal)
Base Exp. 165
Growth Rate Medium Fast

Breeding

Egg Groups Amorphous, Water 1
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Base stats

HP 109
328 422
Attack 66
123 254
Defense 84
155 293
Sp. Atk 81
150 287
Sp. Def 99
182 326
Speed 32
62 179
Total 471 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Evolution chart

  • Stunfisk does not evolve.

Pokédex entries

Stunfisk

Black Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.
White It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.
Black 2
White 2		 When its opponent can’t be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away.
X
Omega Ruby		 Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.
Sword Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.
Shield For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.

Galarian Stunfisk

Sword Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.
Shield Its conspicuous lips lure prey in as it lies in wait in the mud. When prey gets close, Stunfisk clamps its jagged steel fins down on them.

English Stunfisk
Japanese マッギョ (Maggyo)
German Flunschlik
French Limonde
Italian Stunfisk
Spanish Stunfisk

Chinese (Simplified) 泥巴鱼
Chinese (Traditional) 泥巴魚

Name origin

stun
to immobilize temporarily
fish
marine creature
disk
flat circular shape
